title: Batch JSON-array support for bee_cells add (all-or-nothing) + prose steering to one batched heredoc per slice
action: Split addCell (templates/lib/cells.mjs:74) into validate + write phases; add addCells(root, cells[]) that validates every cell (including duplicate ids within the batch and against disk) before writing any. Teach bee_cells.mjs add to accept a JSON array via --stdin/--file alongside the single object; report every added id. Copy templates to .bee/bin siblings (byte-identical). Update the three prose sites (SKILL.md:92, planning-reference.md:146, routing-and-contracts.md:229) to instruct one batched add --stdin heredoc per slice. Add tests: batch happy path, mid-batch failure writes nothing, in-batch duplicate id refuses, single-object path unchanged.

truths: a JSON array piped to add --stdin creates every cell in one invocation
a batch with one invalid cell writes zero cell files
a single JSON object still adds exactly as before
templates/*.mjs and .bee/bin siblings stay byte-identical (existing suite guard)
prohibitions: no change to the cell schema
no partial writes on batch failure
no removal of the single-object input shape

trace.outcome: bee_cells add --stdin/--file accepts a whole-slice JSON array with all-or-nothing validation; single-object path unchanged; planning prose steers slices to one batched heredoc
trace.verify_output: 220 lib checks passed 0 failed (5 new batch-add checks incl. vendored byte-identical guard); onboard suite PASS failures:0 skipped:1
